 Saab utvecklar, tillverkar och underhåller avancerade system inom flygteknik, vapen, ledningssystem, sensorer och undervattenssystem. Saab har sitt huvudkontor i Sverige men en global verksamhet där Saab är en del av många nationers försvarsförmåga. Läs mer om oss här.
   

  

 Vänligen observera att vi arbetar med löpande urval och tjänsten kan komma att tillsättas innan sista ansökningsdatum har gått ut.

title: GRC - Cyber Security Consultant
description:
Nu söker vi fler kollegor till vårt växande Cyber Security-team på HiQ. Här finns möjligheten att tillsammans med ett starkt team hjälpa intressanta företag att leva i en säkrare digital framtid. På HiQ arbetar vi i framkant med cybersäkerhet, under en nationell satsning inom organisationen bygger vi upp ett Center of Excellence i Stockholm. Vårt mål är att etablera en stark cybersäkerhetsleverans som skapar trygghet inom organisationer och företag.
   

  

 På HiQ finns en möjlighet att vara med och bygga något stort. Det är en lärorik och rolig resa vi har framför oss där vår spelplan är enorm och du kan göra ordentliga avtryck i massor av olika bolag och branscher. Vi kommer värdera din erfarenhet och dina tankar om hur vi på bästa sätt kan bygga vidare området. Vi har en bred kundbas där du får möjlighet att testa olika branscher och får uppdrag hos både kunder med ett väletablerat cybersäkerhetsarbete samt hos kunder i kritiska faser där vår erfarenhet är helt avgörande.
   

  

**Vi söker dig som har:**
* Flerårig erfarenhet inom cybersäkerhet, t.ex. inom något av följande:
+ Governance, Risk & Compliance (GRC)
+ Identitets- och behörighetsstyrning (IAM)
+ Informationssäkerhetshantering
+ Standarder som DORA, CRA, NIS2 m.fl.


**Om HiQ**
 På HiQ välkomnar vi alla typer av kollegor – det viktigaste är att du gillar utmaningar och är en schysst lagspelare. Vi består av utvecklare, arkitekter, agila projektledare, UX/UI-designers, cyberspecialister m.fl. – en härlig mix av kompetenser, perspektiv och personligheter som stärker oss varje dag. Vi växer ständigt med ny energi, och här får du både utveckla och utvecklas. Du får förtroendet att prova först och lära sedan, och vi bygger HiQ tillsammans – ett bolag vi själva vill jobba på.
   

  

 Vi tror på att vara riktigt bra på det vi gör, men också på att ha jäkligt kul längs vägen. Vår kultur är drivande – inte styrande – vilket gör att du kan vara dig själv till 100%. Det gör att vi tar vårt jobb seriöst, men inte alltid oss själva. Och vi menar det när vi säger att bra stämning leder till bra resultat.
   

  

*I denna rekrytering tillämpar vi löpande urval och tjänsten kan komma att tillsättas innan ansökningstiden har gått ut. Varmt välkommen med din ansökan!*

title: Senior Backend Engineer - Observability Ingest - Kubernetes Operator/Helm Chart | Sweden | Remote
description:
Grafana Labs is a remote-first, open-source powerhouse. There are more than 20M users of Grafana, the open source visualization tool, around the globe, monitoring everything from beehives to climate change in the Alps. The instantly recognizable dashboards have been spotted everywhere from a NASA launch and Minecraft HQ to Wimbledon and the Tour de France. Grafana Labs also helps more than 3,000 companies - including Bloomberg, JPMorgan Chase, and eBay - manage their observability strategies with the Grafana LGTM Stack, which can be run fully managed with Grafana Cloud or self-managed with the Grafana Enterprise Stack, both featuring scalable metrics (Grafana Mimir), logs (Grafana Loki), and traces (Grafana Tempo).



We’re scaling fast and staying true to what makes us different: an open-source legacy, a global collaborative culture, and a passion for meaningful work. Our team thrives in an innovation-driven environment where transparency, autonomy, and trust fuel everything we do.



You may not meet every requirement, and that’s okay. If this role excites you, we’d love you to raise your hand for what could be a truly career-defining opportunity.

**This is a remote opportunity and we would be interested in applicants based in Spain, Germany, the UK or Sweden at this time.**


**The Opportunity:**
--------------------

  

  

Grafana Cloud is our composable observability platform that integrates metrics, logs, and traces with Grafana. It allows our customers to leverage the best open source observability software – including Prometheus, Mimir, Loki, and Tempo – without the overhead of installing, maintaining and scaling their own observability stack



The Grafana Cloud Observability department is focused on enabling developers to understand the health and performance of their applications and infrastructure in any environment by providing tools to instrument their code, ingest observability data into Grafana Cloud and visualize and explore it.



In this role, you will be part of the Ingest team that develops the software that helps our users to get Grafana's observability stack up and running and keep it working over time. We build and maintain the configuration and deployment tooling that bridges the gap between "I want observability" and "I have observability,". To achieve this we are responsible for projects like the Kubernetes Monitoring Helm Chart, Fleet Management and the Instrumentation Hub.



If you care deeply about developer experience, love the challenge of making complex distributed systems approachable, and want your work to have a direct, visible impact on thousands of users, this role is for you.

  


**What You’ll Be Doing:**
-------------------------



You will bring your expertise to develop and maintain the tooling that powers the ability of our customers to deploy and operate the tooling to ingest observability data into the Grafana stack. This includes


* Design, build and maintain user-facing tools that support the deployment, configuration and operation of Grafana Alloy, the OpenTelemetry Collector, and related components. As of today this enables 1.8 million running instances to send data to Grafana Cloud.
* Own and evolve our Helm chart ecosystem run on more than 10k clusters and 300k nodes, writing charts that are robust, well-tested, and a pleasure for users to use
* Build and maintain tooling that works across platforms: Kubernetes-first, but rock-solid on Linux, Windows, and macOS, too
* Support infrastructure-as-code integrations using ArgoCD, Terraform, Flux, and other GitOps utilities
* Collaborate closely with support, product, and field engineering to understand real customer pain points and translate them into durable tool improvements
* Work in the open: communicate progress transparently, review peers' code thoughtfully, and help raise the technical bar for the whole team
* Contribute upstream to open source projects including Grafana Alloy, OpenTelemetry, and Helm community charts
* Represent Grafana Labs in meetups, and conferences and social media forums


We invest heavily in developer productivity. You can use modern AI coding assistants as part of your daily workflow (your choice of tools, within security guidelines), backed by a company-funded usage budget so you can iterate quickly without unnecessary friction.  

We encourage pragmatic AI-assisted development: faster prototyping, test generation, refactors, documentation, and incident follow-ups—always paired with strong code review and quality standards.  

You’ll also have access to frontier models (e.g., GPT-Codex 5/3, Claude Opus 4.7, Gemini 3 Pro).


**What Makes You a Great Fit:**
-------------------------------



At Grafana Labs, we expect you to be **comfortable working in a remote-first culture**; communication is key. For us, working together means being collaborative, friendly, kind, and respectful. We operate by consensus, you are expected to contribute to a discussion, and commit to the team's decision.


* You believe that great tooling is a force multiplier and you take pride in boosting the experience of the person on the other end of the tooling you build and maintain
* You're a strong communicator, clear in code review, helpful in customer-facing issues, and comfortable working async across time zones
* You love writing tests and treat them as a core part of the design process, not an afterthought
* You're comfortable going deep on a platform problem, whether that's a Kubernetes scheduling edge case, a Windows path separator bug, or a Terraform provider quirk
* You engage with the open source community and care about the health of the projects you depend on
* 4 years of professional software engineering experience, with at least one major language (Go preferred; Python, Rust, and others welcome)
* Hands-on Kubernetes experience: deploying, operating, and debugging workloads in production environments, including familiarity with Helm and the Kubernetes resource model
* Experience building or maintaining tooling for external users or customers - you've thought carefully about API/UX design, backward compatibility, and upgrade paths
* Comfortable writing and maintaining automated tests at multiple levels - unit, integration, and end-to-end

**Bonus Points For:**
---------------------


* Direct experience with Grafana Alloy or the OpenTelemetry Collector - configuration, pipeline design, or contributing upstream
* Experience authoring or maintaining Helm charts, especially in a community or production context
* Experience with GitOps tooling, including Terraform, ArgoCD, or Flux
* Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA) or Certified Kubernetes Application Developer (CKAD)
* Experience building or contributing to developer-facing CLIs or configuration tooling
* Familiarity with the broader Grafana ecosystem: Loki, Mimir, Tempo, or Pyroscope
* A history of open source contributions with evidence of community collaboration


In Sweden, the compensation range for this role is SEK 775,444 - SEK 930,533. Actual compensation may vary based on level, experience, and skillset as assessed throughout the interview process. All of our roles include Restricted Stock Units (RSUs), giving every team member ownership in Grafana Labs' success. We believe in shared outcomes—RSUs help us stay aligned and invested as we scale globally.


* *Compensation ranges are country specific. If you are applying for this role from a different location than listed above, your recruiter will discuss your specific market’s defined pay range & benefits at the beginning of the process.*


**Why You’ll Thrive at Grafana Labs:**


* **100% Remote, Global Culture -** As a remote-only company, we bring together talent from around the world, united by a culture of collaboration and shared purpose.
* **Scaling Organization** – Tackle meaningful work in a high-growth, ever-evolving environment.
* **Transparent Communication** – Expect open decision-making and regular company-wide updates.
* **Innovation-Driven** – Autonomy and support to ship great work and try new things.
* **Open Source Roots** – Built on community-driven values that shape how we work.
* **Empowered Teams** – High trust, low ego culture that values outcomes over optics.
* **Career Growth Pathways** – Defined opportunities to grow and develop your career.
* **Approachable Leadership** – Transparent execs who are involved, visible, and human.
* **Passionate People** – Join a team of smart, supportive folks who care deeply about what they do.
* **In-Person onboarding** - We want you to thrive from day 1 with your fellow new ‘Grafanistas’ to learn all about what we do and how we do it.
* **Balance is Key** - We operate a global annual leave policy of 30 days per annum. 3 days of your annual leave entitlement are reserved for Grafana Shutdown Days to allow the team to really disconnect. *\*We will comply with local legislation where applicable.*


**Equal Opportunity Employer:** We will recruit, train, compensate and promote regardless of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, disability, age, veteran status, and all the other fascinating characteristics that make us different and unique. We believe that equality and diversity builds a strong organization and we’re working hard to make sure that’s the foundation of our organization as we grow.


*Grafana Labs may utilize AI tools in its recruitment process to assist in matching information provided in CVs to job postings. The recruitment team will continue to review inbound CVs manually to identify alignment with current openings.*



#LI-Remote



*For information about how your personal data is used once you’ve applied to a job, check out our* *privacy policy**.*
company_description: Grafana Labs is the company behind Grafana, the leading open source software for visualizing time series data.

title: Junior Business Analyst inom MS Dynamics!
description:
Är du på jakt efter en utmaning inom IT och vill arbeta i en roll där du får kombinera verksamhetsförståelse med teknisk plattformskompetens? Hos vår kund, ett globalt industribolag med starkt fokus på digitalisering, får du vara med och utveckla en verksamhetskritisk lösning som används världen över. Välkommen in med din ansökan!
  
  

**Om tjänsten:**  

I rollen som Business Analyst kommer du att arbeta i ett team som utvecklar och vidareutvecklar en global Case Management-lösning baserad på Microsoft Dynamics 365. Systemet används av supportorganisationer världen över för att hantera tekniska ärenden kopplade till fordon och serviceprocesser, där både verkstäder och återförsäljare skapar ärenden som sedan hanteras av olika supportnivåer.
  
Du kommer att arbeta nära både verksamhet och teknik, där din främsta uppgift är att analysera behov och översätta dem till lösningar i MS Dynamics. Rollen innebär att du driver dialoger med nyckelpersoner i organisationen, bryter ner krav, föreslår arbetssätt och ser till att lösningen byggs så standardnära som möjligt. Rollen passar dig som trivs i gränslandet mellan system och verksamhet och som vill vara med i en spännande resa där du får möjlighet att påverka arbetssätt, struktur och framtida teamuppbyggnad.
  
  

**Vi söker dig som:** Har en kandidatexamen i systemvetenskap eller datekonomi.
  
Har erfarenhet av kravinsamling, behovsanalys och processkartläggning.
  
Har ett tekniskt intresse och god kommunikativ förmåga.
  
Har ett affärskritiskt tänk och drivs av att skapa värde för organisationen.
  
Kommunicerar obehindrat på engelska i tal och skrift.
  
  

**Det inte nödvändigt, men meriterande om du har:**  

Erfarenhet av en liknande roll vid sidan av dina studier.
  
God förståelse och kunskap i Microsoft Dynamics 365.
  
Erfarenhet av ärendehanteringssystem som ServiceNow eller liknande system.
  
Erfarenhet av att arbeta i globala organisationer med flertalet intressenter.
  
  

För att trivas i rollen tror vi att du är kommunikativ och trygg i dialog med verksamhet och olika intressenter, nyfiken och analytisk med en naturlig förmåga att snabbt sätta dig in i nya behov. Du är lösningsorienterad med fokus på hur behov bäst kan realiseras i plattformen samt trivs med samarbete i gränslandet mellan verksamhet, UX och teknik.
  
  

**Om anställningen:**  

Detta är ett konsultuppdrag vilket innebär att du kommer att vara anställd av Friday och arbeta som konsult ute hos vår kund. Ambitionen är att du på sikt ska gå över i anställning hos kunden.
  
  

**Övrig info:** Omfattning: Heltid.
  
**Placering:** Göteborg.
  
**Start:** September.
  
**Rekryteringsansvarig:** Hanna Lejon.
  
**Lön:** Marknadsmässig månadslön.
  
  

Ansök genom att klicka på länken nedan. Kom ihåg att vara snabb med din ansökan då vi gör löpande urval av kandidater och att annonsen kan stängas ner innan tjänsten är tillsatt om vi gått över till urvals- och intervjufasen. Vi tar inte emot ansökningar via mail.
  
  

**Om Friday:**  

På Friday brinner vi för att ge dig som Tech-talang en riktigt bra start på karriären. Genom att lyssna på vad just du vill och drivs av, matchar vi dig med företag och möjligheter som får dig att se fram emot att gå till jobbet.
  
Vi värderar ambition och potential högt och arbetar aktivt för en fördomsfri rekrytering, där alla ges samma chans att lyckas.
  
Vi finns i Stockholm, Göteborg, Malmö, Linköping och Örebro. Varje år genomför vi över 5 000 karriärmöten och matchar kandidater med allt från globala företag till innovativa startups.

title: E-commerce Performance Analyst - Stockholm
description:
We are seeking a Marketing Analyst to support and improve e-commerce performance by driving online sales, boosting conversion rates, and elevating the customer experience across multiple markets. The role focuses on optimizing product assortments, pricing strategies, promotional activities, and the overall user journey, while also supporting local teams in deploying and using the e-commerce platform effectively.
  
You will work hands-on with data—analyzing performance, building dashboards, and uncovering insights to enhance marketing effectiveness. A key part of the role is understanding customer behavior, segmenting audiences, and contributing to stronger retention and customer lifetime value.
  
You will collaborate closely with both global and regional teams to ensure alignment between strategy and execution. Success in this role requires a structured, analytical mindset, a strong focus on results, and the ability to communicate clearly in an international environment.
  
Qualifications
  
  

Bachelor’s degree in marketing, business, analytics, or a related field
  
  

5+ years of experience in marketing analytics, e-commerce, or business analysis
  
  

Strong analytical skills with the ability to translate data into actionable insights
  
  

Experience working in international or cross-market environments
  
  

Technical skills
  
  

**Tools:** Google Analytics, Tableau, Power BI, Excel
  
  

**Data:** SQL, data visualization, statistical analysis
  
  

**E-commerce:** funnel analysis, user experience (UX), conversion rate optimization (CRO)

**End Date:** 2027-05-01

title: Senior UX-designer Malmö (offentlig sektor)
description:
### **Om uppdraget**

Vi söker en senior UX-designer för ett uppdrag i Malmö med fokus på att utveckla användarvänliga och tillgängliga digitala lösningar. Du blir en viktig del i arbetet med att utveckla digitala lösningar inom offentlig sektor, med höga krav på kvalitet, tillgänglighet och struktur.

### **Dina arbetsuppgifter ️**

* Ta fram och dokumentera designförslag för digitala gränssnitt (koncept till detalj)
* Visualisera och kommunicera designlösningar tydligt för olika målgrupper
* Delta i projekt som rådgivare och bollplank kring UX och design
* Samarbeta nära utvecklingsteam för att säkerställa implementation
* Arbeta enligt etablerat designsystem och varumärkesriktlinjer
* Säkerställa hög användbarhet och tillgänglighet enligt gällande lagkrav

### **Förväntade leveranser**

* Tydlig presentation av UX-arbete till stakeholders

### **Din profil**

* Dokumenterad portfolio av tidigare arbete
* Erfarenhet av Figma
* God kunskap om lagen om digital tillgänglighet (DOS-lagen)

### **Personliga egenskaper**

* Kommunikativ och pedagogisk

title: Product Marketing Manager
description:
The Product Marketing manager will be a key piece to work alongside our VP Marketing and incoming Growth Marketing Lead in a brand new marketing team set-up for Verified.


We need someone to own the Verified narrative. You’re the bridge between what Verified builds and what the market sees. It requires a high level of ownership and the ability to jump between long-term strategy and rapid execution.


This is a blank canvas opportunity for someone who wants to drive change and build a function from zero.


**Responsibilities**

* Product Partnership: You will spend a lot of time with the product team. Your job is to understand the "why" behind our tech and translate it into a clear, competitive narrative.
* Creative Content: We need someone who can craft technical messaging that builds trust, but also unexpected and engaging content that cuts through the noise. You should be comfortable jumping between formats - from deep-dive whitepapers to quirky social ideas.
* Automation & AI: We believe in working efficiently. You’ll be expected to use AI and automation to scale your output, ensuring we spend our time on creativity and strategy rather than manual tasks.
* Sales Support: You’ll help our sales team win by creating the decks, battlecards, and tools they need to communicate our value clearly.
* Creative Problem Solving: Use your creative muscles to find "non-obvious" ways to reach our ICP. Whether it’s a unique content format or a clever distribution play, you think outside the standard B2B playbook.

**Job qualifications and requirements**

* 4–5 years of experience in a similar position within B2B SaaS or a similar fast-paced environment. You’ve ideally been through a startup journey before and know how to deliver when there is no existing playbook.
* A Versatile Crafter: You can switch between formal, technical authority and a more engaging, human tone. You aren't afraid to try unexpected creative ideas to get attention.
* A Pragmatic Builder: You’re happy to be strategic, but also equally enthusiastic about hands-on, crafting and building things.
* Product-Minded: You have a natural curiosity for how things work and how they solve actual problems for real people. Collaborating closely with Product and UX teams.
* AI-Fluent: You’ve already integrated AI into your tech stack to make yourself faster, more creative and efficient. You understand the power and have the ability to automate tasks and workflows so you can focus on more important aspects of your day-to-day.
* Customer Focused: You are obsessed with understanding customers, driven by their real pain and needs, and can articulate their problems better than they can themselves.
* Communication: Exceptional verbal and written communication skills, including presentation abilities.

**Why Join Verified?**


The Journey: We are on a high-growth trajectory, backed by Verdane, targeting a massive market with high ambitions.


The Impact: With Product and Content Marketing as a blank canvas, you'll have the freedom and responsibility to build a high-performing engine and drive real change.


Culture of Excellence: We value builders. If you thrive on autonomy, technical mastery and the challenge of scaling a technical product, you’ll find your home here.

title: Innovation Lead / Senior Project Manager R&D Getinge
description:
Innovation Lead / Senior Project Manager R&D Getinge


**Date:** Apr 2, 2026
**Location:**Solna, SE
**Company:** Maquet Critical Care AB
**Remote Work:****With a passion for life**  

Join our diverse teams of passionate people and a career that allows you to develop both personally and professionally. At Getinge we exist to make life-saving technology accessible for more people. To make a true difference for our customers – and to save more lives, we need team players, forward thinkers, and game changers.
Are you looking for an inspiring career? You just found it.  



We are looking for an Innovation Lead / Senior Project Manager to drive innovation projects from early concept to validated solutions ready for R&D development and implementation.

This role sits at the interface of innovation, R&D, and business, ensuring ideas are matured, tested, and structured so they can be successfully taken forward into formal development.  


**What you will do**
* Lead end-to-end innovation and early-phase development projects, from pre-study and concept to validated and transferable outcomes
* Drive technical pre-studies and feasibility investigations for next-generation products and functions
* Lead concept development, rapid prototyping, and iterative design cycles
* Translate exploration into clear actions, priorities, decisions, and concrete deliverables
* Apply and further develop Technology Readiness Levels (TRL) to structure maturity and progression
* Build, shape, and lead truly cross-functional teams across R&D, product, and business, ensuring alignment, shared ownership, and clear communication
* Drive complex technical work by actively engaging in and structuring advanced technical problem-solving
* Demonstrate strong technical leadership, including clear decision-making in uncertain and complex environments
* Facilitate cross-functional teams of experts and lead in a complex organization
* Ensure quality, regulatory compliance, and risk management throughout all phases
* Drive and ensure successful handover into R&D development, including ownership of process alignment, ways of working, and readiness for implementation

  


**Who you are**

You combine technical depth with strong execution capability and are comfortable working in complex, regulated environments. You bring strong technical leadership, with the ability to understand, challenge, and guide advanced technical topics while enabling teams to deliver. You are comfortable making decisions and driving progress in complex environments  


**What you bring**
* MSc or PhD in Engineering or a relevant field
* 8+ years of experience from medtech R&D or other regulated industries, preferably medical device or critical care products
* Solid experience in product development of complex, integrated (embedded) systems, with strong understanding of the full development lifecycle
* Proven experience leading cross-functional technology development
* Strong stakeholder management and cross-company collaboration skills
* Experience working as a facilitator of experts
* Experience or strong understanding of TRL frameworks is highly valued
* Fluency in Swedish and English, written and spoken


Great if you also have experience in:
* Concept development and rapid prototyping
* Iterative design and design reviews
* Customer workflows and UX validation
* Experience or strong understanding of simulation and modeling

  


**What makes you successful**

You demonstrate strong technical leadership and the ability to go deep into complex technical domains when needed. You turn ideas into structured outcomes that others can build on. You combine a pragmatic approach with strong structure and methodological rigor and a strong focus on quality and continuous improvement.  


**Why this role matters**

You will play a key role in ensuring that innovation efforts result in robust, innovative solutions that can be successfully developed into future products within Critical Care.  


**Application**

Applications will be reviewed continually, and we therefore recommend sending us your application as soon as possible, but no later than 2026-05-02

We kindly ask you to submit your application in our recruitment system by clicking the “apply now” button. Due to GDPR regulations we do not accept applications via e-mail.

If you have any questions regarding the role, please contact Hiring Manager Olga Malmberg at olga.malmberg@getinge.com or Talent Partner Emma Paulsson at emma.paulsson@getinge.com.

We look forward to receiving your application. We hope you will join us on our journey to become the world’s most desired MedTech company.  


**About us**With a firm belief that every person and community should have access to the best possible care, Getinge provides hospitals and life science institutions with products and solutions aiming to improve clinical results and optimize workflows. The offering includes products and solutions for intensive care, cardiovascular procedures, operating rooms, sterile reprocessing and life science. Getinge employs over 12,000 people worldwide and the products are sold in more than 135 countries.  


Reasonable accommodations are available upon request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process.
company_description: Getinge is a global leading supplier of innovative medtech products and solutions within healthcare and life science.

title: UX/UI Designer Intern
description:
**XR Solutions** 




**UX/UI Designer Intern — XR / AR Platform (Remote)** 




  





**About us** 




 We build augmented reality systems for industrial clients. Our software runs on factory floors and in production environments, guiding technicians through maintenance, inspection, and operations on real machinery. The common thread across our work: AR content has to stay precisely locked to real-world equipment, reliably, in environments that weren't designed for it.
 



  





**The role** 




 This is not a “support” design role.
 



  





 You will take ownership of the UX/UI for our XR glasses platform — designing interfaces that technicians rely on in real working environments. This includes everything from step-by-step workflows to live AR collaboration tools and real-time data overlays.
 



  





 You’ll work across the full design process, from early research to production-ready design, in close collaboration with product and engineering.
 



  





 Remote to start, with the option to move hybrid later.
 



  





**What you’ll do** 



* Own the UX/UI design process end-to-end (research → concept → prototype → delivery)
* Design for XR environments (spatial UI, voice, gestures, eye tracking)
* Conduct user research and usability testing with real industrial users
* Build and evolve design systems in Figma
* Work closely with developers (Unity) to bring designs into production



  





**What we’re looking for** 



* Strong working experience in Figma (you know your way around systems, not just screens)
* Experience building and maintaining design systems
* Ability to work independently and make decisions without constant direction
* Clear, structured thinking and attention to detail



  





**Nice to have** 



* Experience with AR / XR / spatial design
* Experience working with Unity or similar environments



  





**Important to know** 



* This is an
 **unpaid internship**
* High-performing interns are considered for a
 **paid role**



  





 We aim to make this worth your time:
 



 You’ll work on a live product, solve real problems, and gain experience in a space most designers don’t get access to early in their careers.
 



  





**What you’ll gain** 



* Hands-on experience designing for XR hardware
* Exposure to industrial use cases (not just consumer apps)
* A strong, real-world portfolio project
* Potential pathway to a full-time role



  





 Interested? Send us your CV. We'd love to hear from you.

title: Visual Designer
description:
We are looking for a Visual Designer with a strong eye for detail and a solid understanding of retail design. Someone who can turn creative ideas into clear, functional and well-crafted visual executions across channels. If this sounds like the next step in your career, apply now!
   

  

**About The Role**
 As a Visual Designer at Fjällräven, you’ll be part of the Creative Operations team, working closely with global designers, copywriters and creative leads to bring cohesive brand experiences to life. Your focus will be on translating ideas into tangible, high-quality retail executions, both in physical spaces and across digital touchpoints. From seasonal campaigns to in-store storytelling, your work will bridge brand and commerce in a way that feels natural, functional and enduring.
   

  

 This role is hands-on and collaborative. You’ll contribute to shaping creative ideas and follow them through to production, ensuring consistency and quality across all outputs. Working across own retail and wholesale environments, you’ll help create connected experiences that reflect Fjällräven’s identity and meet real business needs.
   

  

 The position is based in Stockholm, Sweden and reports to the Global Visual Designer. This is a full-time temporary role for two years.
   

  

**What You Will Do**
*In more detail you will focus on the following:*
* Develop and execute creative ideas for retail marketing content for own retail and retail channels
* Translate seasonal campaign ideas into in-store and digital executions
* Create retail marketing materials such as window posters, signage, POS, shelf labelling and packaging
* Develop content for digital signage, floor stickers and other in-store communication formats
* Collaborate closely with Head of Wholesale, Visual Merchandising and Retail Concept teams to meet retail requirements
* Support creation of campaign guidelines and retail brand experience content
* Work hands-on with design and production from concept to final delivery
* Stay informed on best practices in retail storytelling, content and brand experience


**WHO YOU ARE**
 We think you could be a great match for the position if you possess the following traits and capabilities:
   

  

* A degree in Visual Design, Graphic Design or equivalent practical experience
* 5+ years of experience working with retail marketing design, either in-house or at an agency
* Experience delivering cross-channel campaigns and brand experiences, ideally in outdoor, lifestyle, or sport industries
* Strong visual design skills with a sharp eye for detail and consistency
* Proficiency in tools such as Figma and Adobe Creative Cloud
* A solid understanding of retail design, visual merchandising and user-centered design principles
* A collaborative mindset and ease working across disciplines and teams
* Experience in photo editing, and video editing skills being nice-to-have
* Fluency in English; additional languages are a plus


**HOW TO APPLY**
 Please submit your CV and portfolio through our application portal. The application deadline is 7th of May, and we review applications on an ongoing basis, so we encourage early submissions.
   

  

 This is a full-time temporary position for two years based in our office in Solna. Our office policy allows for one work-from-home day per week; on the remaining days we work from the office.
   

  

 We look forward to your application!

title: Designer - Templates
description:
**About SCRL** 

 SCRL is a mobile-first design platform built for social media and used by millions of creators worldwide.
 



  





 We're a team based in central Stockholm, working closely across functions with a shared commitment to quality, clarity, and craft.
 



  





**Role Overview** 




 In the SCRL app, templates, stickers and frames help users turn their photos into standout carousels. You’ll be designing best-in-class templates, stickers, and frames that combine technical polish with high creative standards.
 



  





 We’re looking for someone who 
 
 lives for the craft of design
 
 . You care deeply about layout, realism, and nailing the perfect texture. You’re excited to experiment with AI tools but just as willing to get hands-on: photographing materials, scanning, or remixing assets to build something authentic.
 



  





 You’ll be 
 
 producing content yourself
 
 , helping set the standard for a new team that’s focused on becoming the best in the world at what we do.
 



  





**Who You Are** 




 You thrive on delivering well-crafted, user-centric content and enjoy working in a fast-paced, collaborative environment.
 



  





 We value your keen eye for photo layout and your coordination skills, as you’ll strongly contribute to the planning and execution of in-app content that inspires and supports our users.
 



  





**What You’ll Do** 



* Produce popular templates:
 
  Research trends, analyze user-generated content, and design story and feed post templates that inspire and resonate with SCRL users.
* Coordinate the creative process:
 
  Handle everything from sourcing materials (stock photos, fonts, etc.) to collaborating with other designers in the team and implementing templates with the product team.
* Build and maintain a dynamic template ecosystem:
 
  Create a living, collaborative library that blends user-generated content with seasonal and trend-driven templates.
* Support key marketing moments:
 
  Plan and execute templates for campaigns like December Rewind and Valentine’s Day, driving engagement and elevating user experiences.



  





**You Should Have:** 



* 3+ years of experience in creative production.
* Strong collaboration skills, with experience working across teams, including marketing, design, and product.
* Proficiency in tools like Figma, Adobe Creative Cloud or similar design software.
* A creative sense and keen eye for photo layout.



  





**Nice-to-Have:** 



* Experience with apps or tools for photographers and creators.
* Understanding of media/photo-sharing in social platforms and its requirements.
* Analytical abilities to assess template performance and guide improvements.



  





**What You’ll Get** 



* Freedom to innovate and explore creative solutions.
* The opportunity to make a direct impact on SCRL’s growth and user engagement.
* A collaborative, supportive team in our Stockholm office.
* Competitive salary and opportunities for professional development.



  





**Ready to Join Us?** 




 We believe creativity should be accessible to everyone. If this resonates with you, we’d love to hear from you.

title: UX/UI-designer inom kraftsystemanalys
description:
**Bakgrund och Uppdragsbeskrivning**
 En av Svenska kraftnäts IT-sektioner behöver förstärkning. Som UX/UI-designer kommer du ansvara för att skapa användarvänliga, visuellt tilltalande och effektiva digitala upplevelser. Du ansvarar för att förstå användarnas behov och omsätta insikter till
   

  

 användarcentrerade designlösningar som skapar värde för både användare och verksamheten. Arbetet omfattar applikationer som hanterar och bearbetare stora datamängder för att möjliggöra planering av det svenska transmissionsnätet inklusive utbyte av data med de nordiska systemoperatörerna samt planering och utveckling av det svenska elnätet. Du kommer, över avtalstiden, att arbeta som UX-designer i projekt eller leveransteam som arbetar inom sektionens ansvarsområde. Teamen är tvärfunktionella och består av ett flertal personer med olika roller såsom produktägare, scrum master, systemutvecklare, testare och kravanalytiker. Arbete
   

  

 drivs i tätt samarbete med användare i form av data scientists och kraftsystemanalytiker, samt även andra intressenter såsom domän- och lösningsarkitekter, projektledare, mfl. Då verksamheten har höga säkerhetskrav arbetar vi primärt med inköpta eller egenutvecklade on prem lösningar alternativt gemensamma nordiska tjänster. Våra egenutvecklade system är utvecklade i C#, Python, C++ och/eller React samt driftas på Windows, Linux eller Kubernetes. Du kommer initialt att arbeta i två leveransteam men under avtalsperioden kan det bli aktuellt att jobba i andra team eller projektgrupper. Idag arbetar vi projektbaserat enligt PPS och leveransteamen som tillhör förvaltningsorganisationen arbetar agilt. Det är viktigt med erfarenhet av agilt UX/UI-arbete
   

  

 med tillhörande metoder och verktyg (t.ex. Azure DevOps, Axure.
   

  

**Arbetsuppgifter**
 Rollen kombinerar användarcentrerad design (UX) med visuellt gränssnitt (UI) och innebär ett nära samarbete med ett flertal andra roller och intressenter. Konsulten driver designprocessen från insikt och idé till prototyp och färdig lösning.
   

  

 Arbetsuppgifter kommer bland annat att innefatta:
   

  

* Genomföra användarundersökningar, intervjuer och användartester.
* Ta fram personas, scenarios, kundresor och användarflöden.
* Identifiera problem, behov och möjligheter genom research och dataanalys.
* Utveckla högupplösta prototyper och visuella koncept.
* Designa komponenter, ikoner och layout enligt modern designpraxis.
* Arbeta med typografi, färgsystem och visuella riktlinjer.
* Säkerställa att designen följer företagets designsystem — eller bidra till att vidareutveckla ett.
* Skapa design som fungerar responsivt och tillgängligt på olika plattformar.
* Arbeta tätt ihop med utvecklingsteam kring implementering av design.
* Presentera designförslag och leda workshops för att engagera team och stakeholders.
* Delta i produktplanering och bidra med designperspektiv i strategiska beslut.
* Följa upp implementationer och säkerställa att designen omsätts korrekt i produkten.
* Genomföra och analysera användartester för kontinuerlig förbättring.
* Säkerställa god tillgänglighet enligt WCAG och andra standarder.

title: Digital Communications and Marketing Trainee, Autumn 2026
description:
As a trainee at Curious Mind, you will work side by side with experienced consultants and sharpen your skills in digital content for different channels. Our workdays are anything but predictable. One day you are shaping a brand’s tone of voice, the next you are supporting a client workshop full of new ideas.
   

  

 To enjoy life as a consultant, you need to appreciate variety — because it’s that constant change that makes the work so rewarding.
   

  

**What we do**
 Curious Mind is a Nordic content partner with global reach. We work with a broad range of clients across a wide spectrum of strategic and operational engagements, including website maintenance and CMS migrations, Content prompt engineering, CRM content, UX writing, project coordination, and sustainability reporting.
   

  

 Want to know more about us? Head over to curiousmind.se and explore.
   

  

**What you can expect as a trainee at Curious Mind**
* A mentor, senior advisors, and a whole team of curious consultants ready to support you every step of the way.
* The chance to create sharp, relevant content in different projects — we always aim to find real client assignments for our trainees.
* The opportunity to grow your skills as a consultant: navigating clients, managing stakeholders, and creating real value.
* A culture built on feedback, coaching, and shared growth.
* A global network of experts in digital communication and transformation through Eidra.


**Who we think you are**
* You are at the end of your studies within media, communication, digital marketing, or a similar field.
* You have an internship, LIA, or trainee period coming up in Autumn 2026.
* You speak both Swedish and English fluently and have a passion for writing.
* You have a curious mindset and want to know more about what it really means to work as a content consultant.
* You are a communicative problem solver and you’re not afraid to ask for help and support when needed.
* Whether it’s AI, smart tools, or structured thinking, you see technology as a way to fuel your creativity.


 Questions? Reach out to Amanda Jernberg at amanda.jernberg@curiousmind.se.
   

  

 Don’t hesitate to apply — interviews are held continuously and spots fill up quickly.
   

  

 Curious Mind is a Nordic content partner with a global reach, fueled by curiosity. Our team of around 50 content consultants helps brands across the Nordics grow through digital communication, with impact that travels far beyond our borders.
   

  

 We’re proud to be part of Eidra, a carefully selected group of specialist companies. While we each have our own craft, we’re united by a shared ambition: to help our clients become leaders in their fields.
   

  

 Our modern offices in the heart of Stockholm are home to a vibrant community, where we work side by side with our sister agencies.

title: Customer Activation Manager - Lifeplan
description:
****Who are Lifeplan?****
 Want to make a real difference to people’s future? At Lifeplan, Sweden’s largest digital pension advisor, we help individuals make smarter decisions about one of their most important investments - their pension. Through a unique combination of advanced technology, research-driven analysis and fully independent advice, we provide tailored pension advice based on each individual’s life situation and goals. Our ambition is simple: to make it easy to do the right thing, and help people maximise the value of their pension savings over time.
   

  

 Lifeplan is a sister company to Benifex, a global SaaS company within employee benefits and total rewards. We share offices and work closely together, giving you the opportunity to be part of a smaller, specialist company while also benefiting from the expertise, network and development opportunities of an international tech organisation.
   

  

****Why should you apply? 🌟****
* Play a key role in shaping how employees in Sweden experience and engage with Lifeplan, directly influencing activation, usage and perceived value of a growing digital service.
* Take on a highly hands-on role with real ownership, autonomy and the opportunity to build and refine activation journeys end to end.
* Work in a fast-moving, cross-functional environment where your ideas, insights and execution genuinely make an impact.
* Join a team that values data-driven decision-making, creativity and pragmatic problem-solving in equal measure.
* Help people make smarter decisions about their pension - fully independent.


**Great Benefits**
 ⚖️ Guaranteed work-life balance with a hybrid working model.
   

  

 🧠 ITP1 pension.
   

  

 ❤️ Lifeplan.
   

  

 💸 Access to the Benifex benefits portal.
   

  

 🤸 Generous wellness allowance.
   

  

 ✈️ 30 vacation days / year.
   

  

 💰 Referral bonuses.
   

  

 🌟 Enhanced parental pay.
   

  

 🙂 Volunteer Days – two paid days annually to give back to the community.
   

  

****Role Overview 🚀****
 As a Customer Activation Manager within Lifeplan, you will own and develop how the Lifeplan service communicates with and activates end users – employees of our customer companies. Your mission is to increase activation, engagement and long-term usage through relevant, measurable and engaging communication across the entire user journey. This role is highly operational and hands-on, with a strong focus on behavioural change, from first exposure to recurring use. You will work closely with commercial, product, customer success and marketing teams in a cross-functional and fast-paced environment.
   

  

****Responsibilities 🤘****
* Own and continuously develop all end-user communication for the Lifeplan service across channels.
* Plan, produce and execute communication and activation initiatives.
* Own and optimise the end-user journey from an activation and communication perspective.
* Work hands-on with key channels, including the benefits portal, email communication, Lifeplan.se, webinars and information campaigns.
* Create engaging communication assets, including copy and simpler visual materials, or brief and quality-assure work produced by others.
* Generate insights from user data and translate them into concrete improvements and actions.
* Monitor, analyse and optimise performance using tools such as Google Analytics and Hotjar.
* Support Account Managers and Customer Success in client dialogues related to employee activation..
* Test, evaluate and help establish new activation and communication channels.


****What are we looking for? 🔎****
* Around 2–5+ years of experience working with activation, user journeys and conversion within B2C, e-commerce, product marketing or digital marketing.
* Hands-on experience with funnels, user behaviour analysis and performance follow-up.
* Proven ability to create content independently, including copy and basic visual materials.
* High digital proficiency and the ability to quickly learn new tools, systems and platforms.
* A hands-on, proactive approach with a strong sense of ownership from idea to delivery.
* Creative judgement combined with resilience and the ability to navigate ambiguity in a fast-changing environment.
* Fluency in Swedish and English.


**Bonus Points For**
* Knowledge of UX/UI principles or web analytics.
* Experience with WordPress and/or Elementor.
* Basic skills in design tools such as Photoshop, Illustrator or similar.
* Experience working with financial products or services.


****Our interview process****
**✌** 
 We understand the need to have a fast and efficient process, the below will all be completed in the shortest time possible.
   

  

 📞 Initial call with the Talent team.
   

  

 ⏳ Interview with a peer.
   

  

 🤩 Final interview with the hiring manager.
   

  

 ⏳References.
   

  

 We want to make the application process as simple and fair as possible. That’s why we don’t ask for a cover letter for this role. Instead, you’ll find a few short questions in the application form. Your answers will help us understand your experience and skills in a more direct and relevant way.
   

  

*Please let us know if you need any reasonable adjustments for interviews by replying to the email you will receive after you apply.*

title: Product Manager (Build Experience)
description:
**TL;DR**
 We're looking for a Senior Product Manager to own the design and visual building experience at Lovable. You'll lead visual editing, content editing, design systems, and theming, all AI-first, making sure that what people build with Lovable is consistent, on-brand, and easy to experiment with.
   

  

**Why Lovable?** 
 Lovable lets anyone and everyone build software with any language. From solopreneurs to Fortune 100 teams, millions of people use Lovable to transform raw ideas into real products - fast. We are at the forefront of a foundational shift in software creation, which means you have an unprecedented opportunity to change the way the digital world works. Over 2 million people in 200+ countries already use Lovable to launch businesses, automate work, and bring their ideas to life. And we're just getting started. We're a small, talent-dense team building a generation-defining company from Stockholm. We value extreme ownership, high velocity, and low-ego collaboration. We seek out people who care deeply, ship fast, and are eager to make a dent in the world.
   

  

**What We're Looking For**
* 6+ years in the software industry, with experience in product management or engineering leadership on design tools, visual editors, or creative platforms (Figma, Canva, Webflow, or similar)
* Deep understanding of design systems, theming, typography, and layout, and a sharp eye for what makes visual output feel professional rather than amateur
* Experience thinking about design in an AI-first context, including how generative AI can produce, adapt, and maintain visual quality at scale
* Technical curiosity: you don't need to code, but you understand how design tools and AI systems work under the hood well enough to make good product calls
* Preferred: Prior experience closing the gap between AI-generated output and genuinely designed experiences; strong opinions on how AI should make design decisions on behalf of users


**What You'll Do**
* Represent the user. Bring findings on design quality, visual editing workflows, and content creation patterns to the team with clarity and conviction
* Run discovery end-to-end: user interviews, competitive research, usability testing, data analysis, and crafting messaging for upcoming features
* Own the design quality bar. Make sure AI-generated outputs are consistent, on-brand, and give users the confidence to experiment freely and ship
* Scope ruthlessly. Ship the right slice of functionality, validate what works through user feedback and metrics, and cut what doesn't
* Enable sales, support, and marketing with the context they need to talk about design features accurately and confidently
* Projects you'll tackle first: Auditing the current state of AI-generated visual output and defining the quality gaps; owning the first iteration of a coherent theming and design system experience; running discovery on the visual editing workflows users rely on most and where they break down


**About Your Application**
 Please submit your application in English. It's our company language, so you'll be speaking lots of it if you join. We treat all candidates equally - if you're interested, please apply through our careers portal.

title: Global Digital Commerce Director
description:
Acne Studios is a progressive luxury house and through collaboration and curiosity, we set high aspirations and strive for excellence. We value everyone’s contribution and embrace feedback to develop ourselves and others. We aim to minimise our environmental impact across all our operations and to address societal challenges in the way we conduct our business.
   

  

 As Global Digital Commerce Director at our Stockholm office, you will shape and lead Acne Studios’ global digital commerce agenda at a critical stage of our growth. This is a senior, high-impact leadership role with global scope, responsible for accelerating revenue, elevating the digital customer experience, and strengthening our omnichannel ecosystem.
   

  

 You will own the global e‑commerce and marketplace business end to end. You will operate at the intersection of brand, culture, technology, and commerce. Always ensuring our digital presence is premium, relevant and unmistakably Acne Studios. You will succeed by navigating a complex organisation, balancing priorities across multiple HQ stakeholders, and partnering closely with regional teams to drive alignment and commercial impact.
   

  

 CULTURE OF COLLABORATION
   

  

 We firmly believe that when you feel supported, included, and trusted, it enables you to enjoy your work and thrive in it. You will be a part of a team of colleagues with diverse roles and backgrounds, each with their unique skills and experiences.
   

  

 WHAT YOU WILL DO
   

  

 As Global Digital Commerce Director, your main contribution will be to set the long‑term vision and lead the evolution of Acne Studios’ global digital commerce and marketplace business, positioning it as a key growth engine and a benchmark for luxury digital experiences. Reporting to the Chief Customer Officer, you will play a vital role in accelerating global revenue growth, strengthening omnichannel excellence, and future‑proofing our digital capabilities by:
   

  

* Defining and leading a global e‑commerce and marketplace strategy that accelerates growth, scalability and long‑term value creation
* Owning the global e‑commerce P&L with full accountability for revenue growth, profitability and commercial performance
* Driving step‑change improvements in UX, site performance, content, and campaigns through advanced analytics, experimentation and customer insight
* Shaping a demand‑led, curated online assortment in close partnership with Buying and Merchandising to maximise relevance and conversion
* Leading performance marketing, CRM, and social commerce to significantly grow customer acquisition, loyalty and lifetime value at scale
* Orchestrating a seamless, premium, and brand‑defining omnichannel customer journey across all digital and physical touchpoints
* Building and deepening strategic partnerships across platforms, technology providers and global marketplaces to expand reach and capability
* Leading, inspiring and scaling a high‑performing global digital commerce organisation with strong commercial and creative capability
* Driving alignment, clarity and momentum across HQ functions and regions within a complex matrix organisation


 WHO YOU ARE
   

  

 You have high aspirations and focus on details that create substantial impact. You enjoy working as a team and you embrace feedback to develop yourself and others. You are curious and thrive in a dynamic environment. Cooperation, respect, and clear communication are important, along with your ambition to learn and grow. If these qualities speak to you, we would love to have you on our team.
   

  

 Specific personal qualities for this role:
   

  

* You are a commercially driven, culturally fluent leader with strong strategic judgement and executional strength
* You thrive in fast-paced, international environments and are skilled at aligning diverse stakeholders around shared goals
* You bring a deep interest in pop culture, digital trends, and contemporary media, and understand how culture shapes brand relevance and consumer behaviour in luxury


 Specific experience and knowledge for this role:
   

  

* Bachelor’s degree in Business, Economics, Marketing, Digital Commerce, or a related field is required
* Master’s degree or MBA in strategy, digital business, or international management (strongly preferred)
* 8+ years’ senior leadership experience in e‑commerce, ideally within luxury or premium consumer brands
* Proven experience operating in complex, global organisations with multiple HQ stakeholders and regional markets
* Strong understanding of omnichannel commerce, luxury consumer behaviour, and global e‑commerce dynamics
* Solid track record in digital marketing, CRM, and performance-led growth
* Experience with platforms such as Salesforce Commerce Cloud, BI tools, PIM, and OMS
* Fluent in English


 What matters most to us is not whether you master all the listed knowledge and skills. We are dedicated to recognising the potential in each individual. We embrace diversity of people as well as ideas and viewpoints and encourage everyone's individual expression of their own identity.
   

  

 A background check will be conducted on the final candidate as part of our standard recruitment process.

title: Project Manager, Consumer Products
description:
**Hands-on Project Manager, Consumer Products at World-Renowned Game Studio** 




  





 We’re looking for a Project Manager / Producer who is great at keeping multiple projects moving, creating structure, following up, and making sure things actually get done. You would work across physical consumer products, global collaborations, and brand-led projects with many moving parts. A big part of the role is to bring clarity, coordinate stakeholders, and keep momentum across timelines, approvals, documentation and delivery.
 



 This is a hands-on role, close to the day-to-day work for someone who enjoys being the person who keeps things together, spots gaps early, and makes sure projects stay aligned and on track.
 



  





**What you’ll be doing** 



* Coordinate and track several projects from brief to delivery
* Work closely with internal teams, external partners and stakeholders
* Run meetings, follow up on actions and keep momentum across projects
* Manage documentation, status updates, approvals and project flow
* Support budget follow-up together with relevant leads
* Help make sure brand guidelines and deliverables stay aligned



  





**Likely background** 



* Producer, Project Manager, Production Manager, Licensing Manager or similar
* Experience from consumer products, licensing, merchandising
* or from a retail, fashion or publishing environment where branded products, approvals and cross-functional coordination have been a natural part of the role
* Comfortable working in structured, fast-moving global setups with lots of stakeholders
* Used to balancing timelines, priorities and details without losing sight of the bigger picture
* Strong written and spoken English



  





**What will help you succeed** 



* A hands-on mindset
* Confidence in coordination-heavy work
* Good judgement, calmness and follow-through
* Ability to handle many parallel workstreams without losing structure
* Comfort working in tools and systems that support tracking, approvals and collaboration



  





**Probably not the right fit if your background is mainly in** 



* Pure marketing or campaign management
* Digital product, tech, UX/UI or SaaS
* Film, content or event production
* Senior strategic leadership roles far from day-to-day execution
* This role is less about setting high-level strategy, and more about driving progress, creating order and making sure projects move forward in a clear and reliable way.



  





**Practical** 




 This is a temporary role with a 18 month contract, starting as soon as possible. The role is based in Stockholm with a hybrid setup, with three days per week in the office.
 



  





 Ticking the boxes? Send us your application!
 



  





**Please note:** 
 If this job disappears from LinkedIn, it doesn’t necessarily mean it’s gone. We regularly update and rotate our LinkedIn listings. You’ll always find all current roles at
 **weareyourally.se** 
 . Follow us on LinkedIn and keep an eye on our website & instagram so you don’t miss out.

title: Backend Engineer (Python/Go) - Consumer Platform Team
description:
**About Wolt**
 At Wolt, we create technology that brings joy, simplicity and earnings to the neighborhoods of the world. In 2014 we started with delivery of restaurant food. Now we’re building the delivery of (almost) everything and you’ll find us in over 500 cities in 30 countries around the world. In 2022 we joined forces with DoorDash and together we keep on dreaming big and expanding across the globe.
   

  

 Working at Wolt isn’t always easy, but it’s definitely exciting. Here you’ll learn more, build more, and ship more than in most other companies. You’ll be challenged a lot, but also have a lot of fun on the way. So, if you’re a self-starter with drive and entrepreneurial spirit, this could be the ride of your life.
   

  

 Are you excited about building the systems that keep Wolt running for millions of customers every day?
   

  

 We’re looking for a pragmatic, product-minded Backend Engineer with strong skills in Python or Go to join our Consumer Platform team – the group behind the core infrastructure and reliability that make Wolt fast, stable, and scalable.
   

  

 This is a role at the intersection of backend engineering, platform thinking, and reliability. You’ll work on the systems that power critical purchase flows and the foundations that other teams build on, shaping how Wolt performs at scale.
   

  

 What you’ll be doing
   

  

 Day-to-day in this role, you’ll:
   

  

* Build and operate high-throughput backend services in Python and Go
* Work across the full development lifecycle, from system design to deployment and monitoring
* Improve the scalability and reliability of Wolt’s core platform
* Design and develop shared services, internal tooling, and platform-level capabilities used across teams
* Drive cross-team initiatives that raise engineering standards and system resilience
* Collaborate with product teams and other engineering groups to support critical consumer-facing flows


 Why this role
   

  

* Work on the backbone of Wolt: Consumer Platform sits at the core of Wolt’s engineering organization. The systems you build power everything from purchase flows to platform-wide reliability, with direct impact on millions of users.
* An AI-first engineering team: We don't use AI as a productivity add-on - we treat agentic coding as a core engineering discipline. We expect our engineers to build with AI agents, to challenge what's possible when the human-in-the-loop is a multiplier rather than a bottleneck, and to push the boundaries of what a small, focused team can ship.
* High-impact, cross-team work: This team is known for driving initiatives that span across Wolt. Recent work includes scaling the purchase core to support split payments, building API gateway capabilities for critical flows, and improving reliability standards across the platform.
* A small team with high leverage: You’ll work in a focused, experienced team where engineers take ownership, move quickly, and influence both technical direction and platform strategy.


 AI-First Engineering in Consumer Platform
   

  

 We’re building platform teams at the forefront of agentic software development. This goes beyond code generation. We’re interested in engineers who:
   

  

* Design workflows where AI agents assist or own parts of the development loop
* Think critically about what should be automated versus human-reviewed
* Build tooling and abstractions that make AI-assisted backend development safer and more effective
* Stay curious about new models, tools, and approaches, and form strong opinions on what actually works


 If you find yourself asking “how should this system be built with AI in mind?” you’ll fit right in!
   

  

 Our Tech Stack
   

  

 Python, Go, MongoDB, Redis, Kubernetes, AWS, Datadog, CI/CD pipelines
   

  

 What we’re looking for
   

  

* Strong backend fundamentals: Experience building production-grade backend services using Python, Go, or similar languages.
* AI-first development mindset: You use AI tools in your daily workflow and think critically about how they improve how you build, debug, and design systems.
* Experience at scale: You’ve worked on high-traffic production systems and understand the challenges of reliability, performance, and scalability.
* System design thinking: You have solid fundamentals in designing distributed systems and care about efficiency, robustness, and maintainability.
* End-to-end ownership mindset: You take responsibility beyond implementation, from shaping solutions to ensuring systems are stable, observable, and production-ready.
* Ability to work independently and drive projects: You’re comfortable owning work end-to-end, navigating ambiguity, and moving initiatives forward with minimal guidance.
* Collaboration and communication: You work effectively across teams and communicate clearly in multi-team engineering environments.
* Reliability and operational awareness: Experience with observability, on-call practices, or reliability engineering is a plus.
* Platform and tooling exposure: Experience contributing to shared systems, internal tooling, or platform engineering initiatives is a plus.


**About You**
* You've built and shipped real software - products, platforms, APIs, apps - and you know what it takes to keep things running reliably in production. You have opinions about architecture but hold them lightly. You write tests. You read the alerts. You don't leave loose ends.
* What sets you apart right now is that you're genuinely excited about where engineering is going. You've been experimenting with AI agents and agentic coding workflows - not because someone told you to, but because you couldn't help yourself. You think about how to redesign your development loop from first principles, not how to bolt AI onto the way you already work.
* You communicate well, collaborate easily across disciplines, and care about the product as much as the code. You're the kind of engineer who asks "what's the best way to..." and then actually follows through.


 Where you'll work
   

  

 These roles are based in our tech hubs in Helsinki, Berlin OR Stockholm.
   

  

 Next steps
   

  

 We’re reviewing applications on a rolling basis, and the role will be filled as soon as we find the right person, so don’t wait!
   

  

 The hiring process typically includes a conversation with the Talent Acquisition Partner, followed by technical interviews, a team chat, and a final decision.
   

  

 Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion
   

  

 We’re committed to growing and empowering a more inclusive community within our company, industry, and cities. That’s why we hire and cultivate diverse teams of people from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We believe that true innovation happens when everyone has room at the table and the tools, resources, and opportunity to excel.

title: Game Client Framework Developer
description:
**About Us**
 Hacksaw Studios is a Stockholm-based game studio focused on creating innovative, high-quality gaming experiences. With a focus on performance and precision, we bring ideas to life through technical expertise and creative collaboration. We have wide distribution, and our games are played by millions of people every month by players all over the world.
   

  

 We’re now expanding our tech team and looking for skilled Framework Developers who want to help us build and refine the foundation that powers our games.
   

  

 The Role
   

  

 As a Framework Developer at Hacksaw Studios, you’ll work together with our game development teams to deliver the features and tools they rely on. The work includes both building new functionality and maintaining, refining and optimising existing code. You'll think holistically about each feature you implement, how it impacts long term developer productivity, usability and performance across memory, GPU and CPU.
   

  

 The framework team is also responsible for areas such as CI pipelines, end-to-end tests and build scripts (including asset optimization and more). Depending on your interests and skillset there is a wide range of technologies you can dive into. We keep meetings to a minimum and there is a lot of freedom and responsibility in prioritising tasks and defining scopes, perfect for someone who communicates well, enjoys finding the right solution and likes having focused time to get things done.
   

  

**Your Key Responsibilities Include**
* Develop and maintain features, tools, and systems used by our game teams.
* Improve performance, usability, and reliability across our framework.
* Considering long-term implications of implmentations, including developer experience, usability and performance.
* Collaborate with developers and designers to identify and solve technical challenges.
* Participate in reviews and provide feedback on code and implementation.


**What We’re Looking For**
* Experience with one or more programming languages suited for game development, such as C#, C++, TypeScript or Haxe.
* Experience with different game development frameworks with some understanding of their structure and how to handle rendering. Ideally with games targeting web.
* A proactive mindset with strong communication skills and a sense of ownership.


**Nice-to-have**
* Experience developing modules used by other developers.
* Interest in performance optimization.
* Our tech stack includes git, TypeScript, Linux, bash, Bun, Docker, Cucumber, GitLab and AWS. Familiarity with any of those tools are beneficial but not required.
* A personal interest in games and an understanding of the gaming industry.


 What We Offer
   

  

* A modern office in central Stockholm.
* Work with experienced, passionate colleagues in the gaming industry.
* The opportunity to create games with a global audience.
* Real influence, ownership, and personal growth opportunities.
* Wellbeing perks including wellness allowance and regular in-office massage.


 Practical Details
   

  

* Unfortunately, we cannot assist with relocation or visa sponsorship.


 Ready to Join the Game Makers?
   

  

 Apply now! We review applications on a rolling basis.
